items.title: Fix category preservation in sidebar navigation in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that the `?category=` query string is preserved when navigating sidebar items in the documents section. Previously, clicking an article would reset the top-nav highlight to 'Documents', causing confusion. Now, users will remain on the correct category tab, enhancing navigation consistency.

items.title: Finalize Use Cases with 60 Service Stubs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the Use Cases reorganization by adding 60 lean service stubs for six new editor hubs. Each hub now mirrors the structure of existing hubs, ensuring consistency across the platform. Key editors included are Windsurf, Cline, JetBrains AI, OpenCode, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.

items.title: Enhance API Key Management for Model Capabilities in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a per-capability `api_key_env` override feature, allowing different API keys for various model groups. The checker now resolves environment variables for API keys, improving access to models that were previously restricted. Backward compatibility is maintained, ensuring existing configurations function as before.

items.title: New Suno Stubs for Editor Hubs in Backend 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces lean Suno stubs for six new editor hubs: Windsurf, Cline, JetBrains, OpenCode, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI. Each stub provides a concise overview, configuration details, and usage prompts tailored for the respective editors. The content has already been applied to production and will be translated into 17 locales.

items.title: Complete Claude Desktop Service Articles in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds three missing articles for the Claude Desktop service to the Use Cases hub, covering Serp, ShortURL, and Suno. With these additions, the hub now features a total of 12 articles, aligning it with the Claude Code hub. The content is already live in production, enhancing user access to comprehensive service documentation.

items.title: Add zh-CN Sources for 8 Use Cases Hub Articles in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the canonical zh-CN markdown sources for eight 'Use Cases' hub overview articles, ensuring the documentation aligns with the production database. These files are now editable within the repository, and the sync process will maintain consistency with the existing translations. This change enhances accessibility for Chinese-speaking users and supports ongoing content updates.

items.title: Gemini Docs Update: No Changes Needed in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update confirms that the recent documentation changes for Gemini do not require any modifications to the MCPs codebase. A thorough verification showed that there is no Gemini mapping or related MCP package in the repository, ensuring that no server code or configurations are impacted. All systems remain unaffected and operational.

items.title: Enhance routing for gemini-3.5-flash in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces routing for the new `gemini-3.5-flash` model, establishing xiaojingai as the primary source and ephone as a fallback. Key changes include the addition of the model in both configuration files and a normalization rule in the chat handler to ensure consistent handling of future variants. This enhancement aims to improve model accessibility and performance on the platform.

items.title: Exciting New Support for Gemini-3.5-Flash Model in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the newly released `gemini-3.5-flash` model within the Gemini service. Key changes include updated billing rules, display rules, and modifications to the API to incorporate this model. The pricing structure aligns with existing models, ensuring consistency across the Gemini-N flash tier.

items.title: Enhance Error Messaging for Motion Control in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the error handling in the ephone Kling motion-control handler by surfacing the actual upstream 400 error messages. Previously, users encountered a generic error message, making it difficult to identify issues. Now, the handler will log the raw upstream response and provide clearer feedback, enhancing user experience and troubleshooting capabilities.

items.title: Fix certificate issues and enhance K8s integration in Auto Cert 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses three critical bugs related to certificate management in K8s. It ensures that ingress-nginx uses up-to-date certificates by updating all necessary fields in Secrets, adds support for the 'zhishuyun' profile, and expands the namespace coverage for the test environment. Additionally, new helper functions have been introduced to streamline certificate verification and fetching processes.

items.title: Enhance COS Upload Timeout Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces configurable timeout settings for COS uploads in the Platform Service, mirroring changes made in the bananarouter. Key modifications include the addition of environment variables for timeout management and adjustments to the SDK constructor to enforce these timeouts. As a result, stalled uploads will now fail more gracefully, improving overall reliability.

items.title: Enhance COS Image Upload Performance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a bounded request timeout for COS SDK image uploads, ensuring faster responses when COS is unreachable. Additionally, it allows configuration of COS acceleration and custom domains without altering the bucket region. These changes aim to improve the reliability and speed of image uploads.

items.title: Optimized Resource Allocation for PlatformBackend in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update right-sizes the `platform-backend` and 21 test deployments, reducing over-provisioning and freeing up significant resources. The main API server's replicas have been reduced from three to two, and resource requests have been adjusted to better match actual usage. Overall, approximately 0.9 CPU cores and 2.25 GiB of memory have been reclaimed for more efficient scheduling.

items.title: Optimize Resource Allocation for Dify Deployments in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical resource issues in the Dify deployments. The `dify-plugin-daemon` has been adjusted to prevent OOM crashes by increasing memory limits and reducing replicas, while the `dify-api` has been right-sized for CPU and memory requests, significantly reducing over-reservation. These changes enhance stability and efficiency without altering environment or application-level configurations.

items.title: Optimize Resource Requests for WebExtrator Workers in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the resource requests for `webextrator-worker` pods from 1 CPU and 1Gi memory to 100m CPU and 512Mi memory. This change alleviates resource wastage, allowing the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler to efficiently pack more workers onto smaller nodes without impacting application performance. The limits remain unchanged to ensure that workers can still burst as needed.

items.title: Boost CPU Requests for Platform Gateway in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the CPU request for the platform-gateway from 100m to 200m to better match actual usage, reducing throttling and improving performance. The Horizontal Pod Autoscaler will now require fewer replicas, stabilizing at around 15-20 pods instead of 30. Overall, this change aims to enhance responsiveness and efficiency without significantly increasing cluster resource usage.

items.title: Optimize replica count for platform-frontend in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: Reduced the number of replicas for the platform-frontend from 3 to 2 to better align with resource needs. This change will help free up approximately 40m CPU and 128Mi memory, improving overall cluster efficiency. The adjustment ensures high availability across nodes while minimizing resource waste.

items.title: Reduced auth-backend replicas for efficiency in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The number of replicas for the auth-backend has been reduced from 4 to 2, optimizing resource usage in the cluster. This change is based on consistent performance metrics indicating that current traffic does not require the higher number of replicas. As a result, approximately 100m CPU and 512Mi memory will be freed up without impacting functionality.
